content.thinking: Let me start by reading the input Excel file to understand its structure.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('input.xlsx', data_only=False)
print('Sheet names:', wb.sheetnames)
ws = wb['Sheet1']
print('Dimensions:', ws.dimensions)
print('Max row:', ws.max_row, 'Max col:', ws.max_column)
print()

# Print all cells with values, formulas, and fills
for row in ws.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=ws.max_row, max_col=ws.max_column):
    for cell in row:
        if cell.value is not None:
            fill_color = ''
            if cell.fill and cell.fill.fgColor and cell.fill.fgColor.rgb and cell.fill.fgColor.rgb != '00000000':
                fill_color = f' [fill:{cell.fill.fgColor.rgb}]'
            print(f'{cell.coordinate}: {repr(cell.value)}{fill_color} font={cell.font.name} num_fmt={cell.number_format}')
"

input.command:
python3 -c "
import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('input.xlsx', data_only=False)
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Check gridlines, column widths, row heights, merged cells
print('Sheet view:', ws.views)
print('Show gridlines:', ws.sheet_view.showGridLines if hasattr(ws.sheet_view, 'showGridLines') else 'N/A')
for sv in ws.views.sheetView:
    print(f'  showGridLines={sv.showGridLines}')

# Check fills on all cells including empty ones
print()
print('Checking yellow fills on empty cells:')
for row in range(1, 40):
    for col in range(1, 12):
        cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=col)
        if cell.fill and cell.fill.fgColor and cell.fill.fgColor.rgb == 'FFFFFF00':
            if cell.value is None:
                print(f'  {cell.coordinate}: empty but yellow fill')

# Check B37, B38
print()
print('B37:', ws['B37'].value, 'fill:', ws['B37'].fill.fgColor.rgb if ws['B37'].fill and ws['B37'].fill.fgColor else 'none')
print('B38:', ws['B38'].value, 'fill:', ws['B38'].fill.fgColor.rgb if ws['B38'].fill and ws['B38'].fill.fgColor else 'none')

# Column widths
print()
for col_letter in ['A','B','C','D','E','F','G','H','I','J']:
    cd = ws.column_dimensions.get(col_letter)
    if cd:
        print(f'Col {col_letter}: width={cd.width}, hidden={cd.hidden}')
"
